evidence_quotes.quote: I see more and more sofisticated gambling mechanics in videogames, even in those played by children, and this needs to be stopped.
Difficult cancellations of subcriptions is another great point.
Only cancel now and pay a fine.
I see a lot of people in the coments mentioning the practice of videogame publishers to destroy older videogames, and I belive it's a good addition to the list.
Videogames are art. They should be preserved, and we need some regulations for that.

paragraphs.text: I'm glad these points are being addressed, especially the third one about addictive design. I have already learned the hard way, but maybe other people will not have to. I see more and more sofisticated gambling mechanics in videogames, even in those played by children, and this needs to be stopped. Difficult cancellations of subcriptions is another great point. I have recently canceled my Adobe subscription and it was an experience. I wanted to leave it running to the end of what I paid for and than not to renew it, but that was not an option. Only cancel now and pay a fine. I see a lot of people in the coments mentioning the practice of videogame publishers to destroy older videogames, and I belive it's a good addition to the list. Same goes for deleting e-books. If I payed for the product... well, I want the product. Videogames are art. They should be preserved, and we need some regulations for that. I do not want unlimitted support from the developer, thats not realistic, but intentional destruction is wrong. Thank you for fighting for consumer rights.
